title: Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 — Consolidated Amendments (2018–2024)
summary: A single consolidated gazette compilation, published by DGMS on 3 July 2024, bringing together the amendments made to the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 between 2018 and 2024. DGMS issues these changes as one combined document rather than separate year-by-year notifications, so this file is the reference point for the current amended text of CMR 2017.
status_note: Amends CMR 2017; CMR 2017 remains operative.

title: CMR 2017 Amendment — Scale of Appointment of Assistant Manager (Reg. 30)
summary: Amendment to the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 notified as G.S.R. 640(E) on 9 October 2025, revising the scale for appointment of Assistant Managers in coal mines (the Regulation 30 cluster). It updates the manpower-to-management ratios coal mine operators must maintain for statutory supervisory staffing.
status_note: Amends CMR 2017 (Reg. 30); CMR 2017 remains operative.

title: Coal Mines Regulations 2017 Amendment - Gas Testing Competency Certificate (2025)
summary: A DGMS notification (G.S.R. 491(E), dated 22 July 2025) issued under Regulations 12(1) and 12(4) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017, laying down the applicability and eligibility criteria for grant of the Gas Testing Competency Certificate to persons authorised to test for inflammable gas in underground coal mines.
status_note: Amends CMR 2017; CMR 2017 remains operative. (Related 2021 competency bye-laws withdrawn 23 Mar 2026.)

title: Draft OSH&WC (Coal Mines) Regulations, 2026
summary: The Draft Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (Coal Mines) Regulations, 2026, published as G.S.R. 67(E) on 28 January 2026 for a 45-day public consultation. Framed under Section 136 of the OSH&WC Code, 2020 (not the Mines Act, 1952), these draft regulations are intended eventually to replace the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017. IMPORTANT: this is a DRAFT and is NOT in force — CMR 2017 remains the operative law.
status_note: Draft — 45-day public consultation; NOT in force. CMR 2017 remains operative.

title: The Mines Act, 1952
summary: The principal Act of Parliament (No. 35 of 1952) that consolidates the law regulating labour and safety in mines across India, defining key terms and the powers of the Chief Inspector of Mines.
status_note: Subsumed by the OSH&WC Code, 2020 (in force 21 Nov 2025); subordinate rules continue under savings.

title: The Mines Rules, 1955
summary: Rules made under Section 58 of the Mines Act, 1952 (SRO 1421 dated 2 July 1955) governing the general administration of mines, including canteens, notices, forms and duties of managers.
status_note: Continues in force under the OSH&WC Code transition (savings), pending replacement.

title: The Mines Rescue Rules, 1985
summary: Rules (G.S.R. 325(E) dated 29 March 1985) made under the Mines Act, 1952 prescribing rescue-station and rescue-brigade requirements for below-ground mines.
status_note: Continues in force under the OSH&WC Code transition (savings), pending replacement.

title: The Mines Vocational Training Rules, 1966
summary: Rules under the Mines Act, 1952 requiring and prescribing vocational training for persons employed in mines before they are put to work.
status_note: Continues in force under the OSH&WC Code transition (savings), pending replacement.

title: The Coal Mines Regulations, 1957
summary: Regulations (S.R.O. 3419 dated 24 October 1957) made under Section 57 of the Mines Act, 1952, superseding the Indian Coal Mines Regulations, 1926, and governing safety in every coal mine in India (later superseded by CMR 2017).
status_note: Superseded by the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017. Retained for reference.

title: Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961 — Second Class Manager's Certificate candidate list
summary: This file is actually a list of candidates granted the Second Class Manager's Certificate of Competency (Restricted) on examination basis under the MMR 1961, not the Regulations themselves.
status_note: Current safety code for metalliferous (non-coal) mines; analogue reference for coal.

title: The Code on Wages, 2019
summary: The Code on Wages, 2019 consolidates and replaces four earlier laws — the Payment of Wages Act, the Minimum Wages Act, the Payment of Bonus Act and the Equal Remuneration Act. It establishes a universal floor wage, statutory minimum wages, timely payment of wages and bonus, and equal pay, and applies to all establishments including coal mines. The four Labour Codes were brought into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025.

title: The Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: The Industrial Relations Code, 2020 consolidates the Trade Unions Act, the Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act and the Industrial Disputes Act. It governs trade union recognition, standing orders, retrenchment and layoff, grievance redressal, strikes and lock-outs, and dispute resolution machinery. It applies to industrial establishments including mines and was brought into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025.

title: The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020
summary: The OSH&WC Code, 2020 consolidates thirteen labour laws on occupational safety, health and working conditions — including the Mines Act, 1952 and the Factories Act — into a single code. It covers working conditions, welfare facilities, hours of work, and safety obligations of employers, and provides the statutory basis (Section 136) under which the Draft OSH&WC (Coal Mines) Regulations, 2026 are being framed. It came into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025; subsumes the Mines Act, 1952 (subordinate rules continue under savings).
source_filename: The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020.pdf

title: The Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: The Code on Social Security, 2020 consolidates nine social-security laws covering provident fund, employees' state insurance, gratuity, maternity benefit, and the extension of social security to gig, platform and unorganised workers. It applies to establishments including coal mines and was brought into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025.

title: Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules operationalising the Code on Wages, 2019 for establishments under central-sphere jurisdiction. Published on 8 May 2026, they prescribe the manner of fixing minimum wages, floor wage, deductions, payment mechanisms and record-keeping.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.

title: Industrial Relations Code (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The Industrial Relations Code (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules under the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, published on 8 May 2026. They detail procedures for trade union registration, standing orders, works committees, grievance redressal, and notices of change, layoff and retrenchment.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.

title: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The OSH&WC Code (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules under the OSH&WC Code, 2020, published on 8 May 2026. They prescribe working-condition standards, welfare facilities, safety committees, health and working-hours requirements and duties of employers for central-sphere establishments.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.
source_filename: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (Central) Rules, 2026.pdf

title: Social Security Code (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The Social Security Code (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules under the Code on Social Security, 2020, published on 8 May 2026. They set out the administration of provident fund, ESI, gratuity, maternity benefit and social-security coverage for gig, platform and unorganised workers.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.

title: Model Standing Orders, 2026
summary: The Model Standing Orders, 2026 issued under the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 provide the default terms of employment — classification of workers, working hours, attendance, leave, conduct and discipline, and termination — applicable to industrial establishments, including mines, until certified standing orders are in place.

title: Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957
summary: The MMDR Act, 1957 is the central law regulating the development and regulation of mines and minerals other than petroleum and natural gas. It governs the grant of mineral concessions (prospecting licences, mining leases and mineral auctions), royalty and the District Mineral Foundation, and empowers rules on mineral conservation and development. This consolidated version incorporates amendments through 2023.

title: Mineral Conservation and Development Rules, 2017
summary: Rules notified under the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957 prescribing scientific and systematic mining, mining-plan and mine-closure requirements, mineral conservation measures and reporting to the Indian Bureau of Mines, applying to all minerals except petroleum, natural gas, coal, lignite, stowing sand and minor minerals.

title: Minerals (Other than Atomic and Hydrocarbon Energy Minerals) Concession Rules, 2016
summary: The Mineral Concession Rules, 2016 (MCR 2016), made under the MMDR Act, 1957, govern the grant, renewal, transfer and management of mineral concessions such as mining leases and prospecting licence-cum-mining leases. This copy is amended up to January 2026.

title: Mineral (Auction) Rules, 2015
summary: The Mineral (Auction) Rules, 2015, made under the MMDR Act, 1957, set out the procedure for auctioning mineral blocks — the method of competitive bidding, eligibility, technical and financial conditions, and grant of mining leases through auction. This version is amended up to 29 January 2026.

title: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) Notification, 2006
summary: The EIA Notification, 2006 (S.O. 1533(E)), issued under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, makes prior environmental clearance mandatory for listed projects including coal mining. It sets out the screening, scoping, public consultation and appraisal process, categorises projects (A and B), and defines the role of the EAC/SEIAA. It is the core environmental-clearance regime that coal mine expansions and new projects must comply with.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

title: Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023
summary: The Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023, in force from 1 December 2023, are made under the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam (Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, as amended). They prescribe the procedure for prior approval to divert forest land for non-forest use such as mining, including proposal processing, compensatory afforestation and conditions of approval. These Rules replaced the Forest (Conservation) Rules, 2022.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
status_note: In force from 1 Dec 2023.

title: Forest (Conservation) Rules, 2022 (superseded)
summary: The Forest (Conservation) Rules, 2022 (G.S.R. 480(E)) prescribed the earlier procedure for approval of forest-land diversion for non-forest uses including mining. They have been superseded by the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023, which took effect on 1 December 2023, and are retained for reference only.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
status_note: Superseded by the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023 (in force 1 Dec 2023).

title: EIA Notification 2006 — Consolidated Amendments Index
summary: A consolidated index of the amendments to the EIA Notification, 2006, listing the S.O. numbers and dates of the numerous amendments made since 2006. It is a navigational reference to track how the environmental-clearance regime has evolved alongside the principal notification.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

title: Ministry of Coal Mining Plan and Mine Closure Plan Guidelines, 2025 (MPG-2025)
summary: Guidelines issued by the Ministry of Coal on 31 January 2025 consolidating the framework for preparing mining plans and progressive and final mine closure plans for coal and lignite mines, covering approving agencies, plan contents and closure-cost provisioning.

title: Corrigendum to MPG-2025 (26 May 2025)
summary: A corrigendum/revision issued by the Ministry of Coal on 26 May 2025 to the Mining Plan and Mine Closure Plan Guidelines, 2025 (MPG-2025), correcting and clarifying provisions of the principal guidelines.

title: Mining Plan Guidelines, 2019 (closure-cost basis; superseded)
summary: The Ministry of Coal Mining Plan Guidelines dated 16 December 2019 established the mine-closure-cost rate basis (per-hectare provisioning, with a base date of 1 April 2019) underpinning escrow arrangements for coal mines. Though superseded by MPG-2025, they remain the key reference for the closure-cost rate basis.
status_note: Superseded by MPG-2025 (31 Jan 2025); retained for the closure-cost rate basis.

title: Central Electricity Authority (Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2023
summary: CEA notification dated 8 June 2023 (Gazette Part III Sec.4) issued under the Electricity Act, 2003, superseding the 2010 regulations and prescribing safety measures for construction, operation and maintenance of electrical plants and lines.

title: Central Electricity Authority (Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2010
summary: CEA regulations of 2010 made under the Electricity Act, 2003 prescribing safety measures for electric supply and installations (superseded by the 2023 regulations).
status_note: Superseded by the CEA Safety Regulations, 2023.

title: DGMS (Tech)(S&T) Circular 02 of 2025 — Minimum air quantity at Continuous Miner and Longwall panels
summary: DGMS circular dated 17 April 2025 issuing guidelines on the minimum air quantity required at Continuous Miner and Longwall mass-production panels under Regulation 153 of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2025 — Quality and Condition of Explosive Materials
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 01 of 2025, dated 17 April 2025, addresses the quality and condition of explosive materials used in mines, prescribing precautions for their storage, handling and use to prevent misfires and premature blasts.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 05 of 2025 — Underground Coal and Metalliferous Mines Safety
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 05 of 2025, dated 8 October 2025, issues directions on safety in underground coal and metalliferous mines, reinforcing precautions for safe underground working.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 06 of 2025 — Prevention of Fall of Person from Height or into Depth
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 06 of 2025, dated 24 November 2025, prescribes precautions to prevent fall-of-person accidents from height or into depth in mines, including edge protection, fall-arrest arrangements and safe work practices.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2024 — Precautions Against Danger of Inundation
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 02 of 2024, dated 22 May 2024, prescribes precautions against the danger of inundation (water inrush) in mines, including surveys of old and adjacent workings, barriers and advance boreholes. It supports the inundation-related provisions of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 03 of 2020 — Guideline for Scientific Study under Regulation 106
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 03 of 2020, dated 16 January 2020, lays down the guideline for conducting the scientific study of strata behaviour and support required under Regulation 106 of the Coal Mines Regulations, before adopting depillaring or mechanised extraction.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 10 of 2020 — Lightning Protection in Mines
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 10 of 2020 prescribes measures for lightning protection in mines, particularly to prevent lightning-induced initiation of explosives and hazards to surface installations.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 13 of 2020 — Medical Examination (Regulation 31)
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 13 of 2020 addresses the initial and periodical medical examination of persons employed in mines and the certificate of fitness, clarifying obligations under the medical-examination regulations. It is a common compliance reference for occupational-health requirements.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 14 of 2020 — Precautions Against Premature Blast of Site Mixed Emulsion
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 14 of 2020 prescribes precautions against premature blasting when using Site Mixed Emulsion (SME) explosives in opencast mines, covering charging, stemming and safe handling to prevent accidents.

title: DGMS Approval Circular 01 of 2020 — Standards for Man Riding Systems in Mines
summary: DGMS Approval Circular 01 of 2020 adopts standards for Man Riding Systems used to transport persons in mines (including underground man-riding installations), specifying design, safety and approval requirements.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2018 — Diesel Equipment Standards
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 01 of 2018 lays down standards for diesel-operated equipment used in coal and metalliferous mines, covering permissibility, exhaust conditioning and safe use of diesel machinery underground and on surface.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2021 — First-Aid Training and Certification Standards
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 01 of 2021, dated 6 August 2021, prescribes standards for the conduct of first-aid training and the issue of first-aid certificates for persons employed in mines, supporting statutory first-aid and emergency-preparedness requirements.

title: DGMS (Legis) Circular 2019 — Equal Employment Opportunity for Women in Mines
summary: The DGMS Legislative Circular of 2019 (revised) provides for equal employment opportunity for women in mines, including deployment of women in below-ground and opencast workings beyond earlier restricted hours, subject to conditions and safeguards.

title: The Explosives Rules, 2008
summary: Rules (G.S.R. 907(E) dated 29 December 2008) made under Sections 5 and 7 of the Explosives Act, 1884, superseding the Explosives Rules, 1983, and governing manufacture, storage, transport and use of explosives.
status_note: Current explosives rules under the Explosives Act, 1884 (PESO).

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 26 of 2026 (30.06.2026)
summary: Revises the DGMS-approved list of laboratories permitted to test the standard properties of chemical additives used for dust suppression in coal mines, broadening it beyond the four originally named institutions to include other government laboratories and test houses so more manufacturers can participate.

title: Appointment of Appellate Authorities for Standing Orders under Section 32 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2314(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 32 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 appointing the Chief Labour Commissioner (Central) and other specified officers as appellate authorities for standing orders in industrial establishments within their respective territorial jurisdictions, superseding the earlier 2016 notification.
source_filename: Appointing Appellate Authority for Standing Orders under section 32 of IR Code 2020.pdf

title: Appointment of Certifying Officers under Section 2(g) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2313(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 2(g) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 appointing the Additional, Regional and other Central Labour Commissioners as certifying officers to certify standing orders within their specified territorial jurisdictions.
source_filename: Appointing Certifying Officer under section 2(g) of the IR Code 2020.pdf

title: Appointment of Conciliation Officers and Their Jurisdiction under Section 43 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2315(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 43(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 appointing the Chief, Additional, Deputy, Regional and Assistant Labour Commissioners (Central) as conciliation officers to mediate and settle industrial disputes within their specified jurisdictions, superseding the earlier 1990 notification.
source_filename: Appointing Conciliation Officers and specifying area of jurisdiction under section 43 (1) and (2) of IR Code 2020.pdf

title: Appointment of Officers to Impose Penalties under Section 85(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2318(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 85(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 appointing Regional Labour Commissioners (Central) to hold inquiries and impose penalties under sub-sections (3), (5), (10), (11) and (20) of Section 86 for establishments where the Central Government is the appropriate government.
source_filename: Appointing Officers to impose penalty under section 85(1) of the Industrial Relations Code 2020.pdf

title: Commencement of Central Advisory Board Provisions of the Code on Wages, 2019
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 4604(E), dated 18 December 2020) issued under Section 1(3) of the Code on Wages, 2019 bringing into force, from the date of publication, the provisions relating to the Central Advisory Board, including parts of Sections 42, 67 and 69.
source_filename: Bringing into effect the provisions of the Code on Wages, 2019 relating to Central Advisory Board.pdf

title: Coal Mines Pithead Bath Rules, 1959 (updated 22.04.2026)
summary: Rules made under Section 58 of the Mines Act, 1952 requiring the provision and maintenance of pithead bath, locker and sanitary facilities at coal mines, classifying mines into categories A to D by average daily attendance.

title: Code on Wages (Central Advisory Board) Rules, 2021
summary: Rules notified as G.S.R. 143(E) on 1 March 2021 under Section 67 of the Code on Wages, 2019 governing the constitution, functioning and procedures of the Central Advisory Board on wages, including matters under Sections 42, 67 and 69 of the Code.
source_filename: Code on Wages (Central Advisory Board) Rules, 2021.pdf

title: Code on Wages (Jharkhand) Rules, 2021
summary: State rules notified by the Government of Jharkhand (S.O. 01, dated 14 July 2021) under Section 67 of the Code on Wages, 2019, superseding the earlier Jharkhand payment-of-wages and minimum-wages rules and governing implementation of the wage Code within Jharkhand.

title: DGMS (Tech)(S&T) Circular 2 of 2025 — Ventilation (CMR Reg 153)
summary: DGMS Technical (S&T) Circular 2 of 2025 issuing ventilation requirements for coal mines under Regulation 153 of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.

title: DGMS 01/2017 — Strata Monitoring Approval in Coal Mine Longwall Working
summary: Lays down a standard protocol and guidelines for strata monitoring in longwall workings of coal mines to evaluate strata-control effectiveness and support-system performance, formulated from a technical workshop held at DGMS Dhanbad on 20.01.2017.

title: DGMS 01/2021 — Bye-Laws for Grant of Competency Certificates under CMR 2017 and MMR 1961 (Withdrawn 23.03.2026)
summary: Notifies the bye-laws framed by the Board of Mining Examination governing the conduct of examinations and grant of competency certificates under the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 and Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961, including the shift of the Manager's Certificate examination to a Computer Based Test only (since withdrawn 23.03.2026).
status_note: Withdrawn by DGMS gazette notification dated 23 Mar 2026.

title: DGMS 02/2017 — Lock Out and Tag Out Energy Shut-Down Procedures
summary: Recommends lock-out/tag-out energy shut-down procedures for coal mines to prevent electrical accidents caused by unexpected energization or uncontrolled release of energy during work on machines and equipment, especially in underground mines.

title: DGMS 04/2017 — Environmental Monitoring System in Coal Mines
summary: Directs owners, agents and managers of belowground coal mines to provide continuous environmental monitoring systems for combustible and toxic gases (methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and other hydrocarbons) to detect hidden fires in sealed-off areas and prevent explosions.

title: DGMS Approval 02/2017 — Dust Suppression Device Approval for Metalliferous Mines
summary: Brings the approval of dust suppression/prevention devices fitted in drilling and boring equipment used in metalliferous mines under Regulation 124(6)(b)(ii) of the Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961 into a general approval scheme with a finalized standard for their manufacture, testing and use.

summary: DGMS Directorate-General of Mines Safety approval circular No. 103 of 1975 recording the grant of statutory approval (under the Coal Mines Regulations) for a specified item of machinery or equipment for use in coal mines.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 122 of 1976 — Flameproof squirrel-cage induction motor 22 kW (Crompton Greaves)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for a flameproof 22 kW squirrel-cage induction motor by Crompton Greaves for belowground coal-mine use.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 160 of 1978 — Flameproof squirrel-cage induction motor 187 kW (Crompton Greaves)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for a flameproof 250 HP (187 kW) squirrel-cage induction motor by Crompton Greaves for belowground coal-mine use.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 454 of 1997 — Flameproof floodlight type PA-3 (SAIT Mine Line)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for a flameproof floodlight (type PA-3) by SAIT Mine Line for belowground coal-mine use.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 458 of 1999 — Flameproof cable coupler and adaptor up to 6.6 kV (Mine Line)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for a flameproof cable coupler and adaptor rated to 6.6 kV/600 A by Mine Line for belowground coal-mine use.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 581 of 2009 — Intrinsically safe Lodestar System (SAIT Mine Line)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for the intrinsically safe Lodestar System by SAIT Mine Line for belowground coal-mine use.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 612/EHQ (2001) — Flameproof/IS equipment for Side Discharge Loader D137/1000 (Simplex)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for flameproof and intrinsically safe electrical equipment on the crawler-mounted electro-hydraulic Side Discharge Loader (type D137/1000) by Simplex Engineering.

title: DGMS Electrical Approval No. 78 of 1974 — Flameproof squirrel-cage induction motor 50 HP (Crompton Greaves)
summary: Renews under Regulation 181(3) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 the DGMS approval for a flameproof 50 HP squirrel-cage induction motor by Crompton Greaves for belowground coal-mine use.

title: DGMS Legislative Circular 2017 — Mines Rules 1955 Amendment for Ease of Compliance in Maintaining Registers
summary: Communicates the amendment to the Mines Rules, 1955 giving effect to the "Ease of Compliance to Maintain Registers under Various Labour Laws Rules, 2017", which replaces 56 overlapping registers and forms across nine central Acts with just 5 common registers/forms.

title: DGMS Legislative Circular 2019 — Equal Employment Opportunity for Women in Mines
summary: Notifies the Central Government's exemption of women employed above and below ground in mines from Section 46 of the Mines Act, 1952, and requires mine owners to frame and implement a standard operating procedure (per the annexed guidelines) for employing women underground and during night hours in opencast/aboveground mines.

title: DGMS Technical Circular 04/2020 — Prevention of Inflammable Gas Hazards in Belowground Coal Mines
summary: Reviews the adequacy of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 and prescribes measures to safeguard mineworkers against methane ignition and explosion hazards in belowground coal mines, with particular reference to the highly gassy Jharia and Raniganj seams.

title: DGMS Technical Circular 08/2020 — Recommendations of the 12th National Conference on Safety in Mines
summary: Circulates the recommendations of the 12th National Conference on Safety in Mines (New Delhi, 28-29 January 2020) covering electrical safety, information technology in mining, pneumoconiosis/silicosis and dust control, disaster prevention in coal mines, and safety and health of contractual workers.

title: DGMS Technical Circular 2017 — Dust Suppression Device Approval for Coal Mines
summary: Brings the approval of dust suppression/prevention devices fitted in drilling and boring equipment used in coal mines under Regulation 123(6)(b)(ii) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 into a general approval scheme with finalized standards for their manufacture, testing and use.

title: Delegation of Recovery-Certificate Power to Regional Labour Commissioners under Section 59(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2320(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 100 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 delegating to Regional Labour Commissioners (Central), within their jurisdiction, the power under Section 59(1) to issue recovery certificates.
source_filename: Delegation of power of Central Government to RLCs under section 59(1) for issue of Recovery Certificate under the Industrial Relations Code 2020.pdf

title: Delegation of Power to File Court Complaints under Section 87(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2319(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 100 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 directing that the Section 87(1) power to file complaints in courts for certain Chapter XIII offences (under sub-sections (4), (6) and (12) to (19) of Section 86) may also be exercised by specified Central Labour Commissioners within their jurisdictions.
source_filename: Delegation of power of Central Government under section 87(1) under Chapter XIII for filing complaints in the Courts for certain offences under the Industrial Relations Code 2020.pdf

title: Draft Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2025
summary: Draft rules notified as G.S.R. 936(E) on 30 December 2025 under Section 67 of the Code on Wages, 2019, inviting objections and suggestions, and proposed to replace earlier payment-of-wages, minimum-wages and related central rules following commencement of all provisions of the Code.
status_note: Draft superseded by the final Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026 (published 8 May 2026).

title: Draft Industrial Relations Code (Central) Rules, 2025
summary: Draft rules notified as G.S.R. 930(E) on 30 December 2025 under Section 99 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, inviting objections and suggestions, and proposed to replace the Industrial Disputes (Central) Rules, 1957 and the Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Central Rules, 1946 following commencement of all provisions of the Code.
status_note: Draft superseded by the final IR Code (Central) Rules, 2026 (published 8 May 2026).

title: Draft Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (Central) Rules, 2025
summary: Draft rules notified as G.S.R. 934(E) on 30 December 2025 under Sections 133 and 134 of the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020, inviting objections and suggestions, and proposed to supersede several existing safety and welfare rules including the Mines Rules, 1955, Mines Rescue Rules, 1985 and Mines Vocational Training Rules.
status_note: Draft superseded by the final OSH&WC Code (Central) Rules, 2026 (published 8 May 2026).
source_filename: Draft Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (Central) Rules, 2025.pdf

title: Draft Code on Social Security (Central) Rules, 2025
summary: Draft rules notified as G.S.R. 935(E) on 30 December 2025 under Sections 154, 155, 158 and 159 of the Code on Social Security, 2020, inviting objections and suggestions, following commencement of all provisions of the Code.
status_note: Draft superseded by the final Social Security Code (Central) Rules, 2026 (published 8 May 2026).

title: Industrial Relations Code, 2020 - Jharkhand Transfer of Pending Cases Notification
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 5683(E), dated 8 December 2025) issued in connection with Sections 51 and 104 of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, providing for the transfer of cases pending before labour courts and tribunals under the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947 to the corresponding tribunals under the new Code upon its commencement on 21 November 2025.

title: Exemption of Low-Wage Vacancies from Section 139 Reporting under the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2343(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 140(2)(b) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 providing that the vacancy-reporting requirement of Section 139 shall not apply to vacancies carrying total remuneration of less than eleven thousand rupees per month.
source_filename: Notification of the Code on Social Security 2020 in the Gazette of India Part II Section 3 Sub Section (ii) regarding.pdf

title: Executive Officer for Chapter XIII of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2344(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 2(30) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 designating the Joint Director or Director (Employment) overseeing the Career Centre (Central) in the Directorate General of Employment as the executive officer to discharge duties under Chapter XIII of the Code.
source_filename: Notification regarding executive officer for the purposes of Chapter XIII.pdf

title: Appointment of Inspectors-cum-Facilitators under Section 122 of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2356(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 122(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 appointing Social Security Officers, Assistant Directors, Deputy Directors, Regional Directors, Additional Commissioners and Insurance Commissioners as Inspectors-cum-Facilitators for Chapter IV and related provisions of the Code.

title: Interest on Delayed Contributions under Section 127 of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2357(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 127 of the Code on Social Security, 2020 specifying that employers shall pay simple interest at twelve percent per annum on any contribution or amount due from the date it becomes payable until the date of actual payment.

title: Authorisation of Officers to Recover Damages under Section 128 of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2358(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 128 of the Code on Social Security, 2020 authorising Insurance Commissioners, Additional Commissioners, Regional Directors and Deputy Directors to exercise powers and functions relating to recovery of damages under Chapter IV within their assigned jurisdictions.

title: Authorisation to Institute and Defend Legal Proceedings under Section 136(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2359(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 136(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 authorising the ESIC Director General and a range of specified officers to institute suits and other legal proceedings on behalf of the Corporation and to defend proceedings against it before courts and tribunals.

title: Notification of Officers to Initiate Prosecutions under Section 137 of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2361(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 137 of the Code on Social Security, 2020 notifying Insurance Commissioners, Additional Commissioners, Regional Directors, Deputy Directors and Assistant Directors, within their jurisdictions, to regulate the initiation of prosecution proceedings under the Code.

title: Authorisation of Compounding Officers under Section 138(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: Central Government notification (S.O. 2362(E), dated 8 May 2026) issued under Section 138(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 read with Rule 54(1) of the Social Security (Central) Rules, 2026, authorising the ESIC Director General, Insurance Commissioners, Additional Commissioners and Regional Directors to exercise the powers of compounding officer for Chapter IV establishments.

title: Notification under Section 2(26) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (Continued ESI Membership on Crossing Wage Ceiling)
summary: This Ministry of Labour and Employment notification (S.O. 2351(E), 8 May 2026) provides that an employee whose wages cross the notified ESI wage ceiling at any time after the start of a contribution period shall continue to be treated as an employee for the remainder of that contribution period.

title: Notification under Section 44 of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (Medical Facility Scheme for Other Beneficiaries, 2026)
summary: This notification (S.O. 2355(E), 8 May 2026) frames the Medical Facility Scheme, 2026, extending ESI-style medical treatment and care to other beneficiaries and their family members registered under schemes other than the Employees' State Insurance Scheme.

title: Notification under Section 9(2) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (Authentication of ESIC Orders and Decisions)
summary: This notification (S.O. 2354(E), 8 May 2026) specifies that all orders and decisions of the Employees' State Insurance Corporation shall be authenticated by the Director General or other authorised officers such as the Insurance Commissioner, Additional Commissioner, Regional Directors, Joint Directors and Deputy Directors.

title: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (Central) Rules, 2026 (DGMS Copy, 12 May 2026)
summary: This notification (G.S.R. 345(E), 8 May 2026) brings into force the OSH&WC (Central) Rules, 2026 under Sections 133 and 134 of the OSH&WC Code, 2020, superseding numerous earlier rules including the Mines Rules 1955, Mines Rescue Rules 1985, Mines Vocational Training Rules 1966 and Contract Labour Central Rules 1971.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026 (DGMS copy).

title: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (Jharkhand) Rules, 2021 [State rule; mine-relevant]
summary: This Jharkhand Gazette notification (S.O. 05, 27 August 2021) publishes draft state OSH&WC Rules under Sections 134 and 135 of the OSH&WC Code, 2020, superseding earlier Jharkhand rules on factories, building and construction workers, contract labour, inter-state migrant workers and motor transport workers, and invites objections within 45 days.

title: Notification under Sections 136(2) and 138(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (Authorised Officers for Prosecution and Compounding of Career Centre Offences)
summary: This notification (S.O. 2342(E), 8 May 2026) authorises specified officers of the Career Centres — the Director (Employment) and Joint Director (Employment) — to sanction prosecution and compound offences under Chapter XIII of the Code on Social Security, 2020 across all of India.
source_filename: Publication of Notification of the Code on Social Security 2020 in the Gazette of India Part II Section 3 Sub Section (ii) regarding.pdf

title: Notification under Section 139(1) of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (Employer Reporting of Vacancies to Career Centres)
summary: This notification (S.O. 2341(E), 8 May 2026) fixes the date, ninety days after notification of the Social Security (Central) Rules, 2026, from which every public- and private-sector establishment must report its vacancies to the concerned Career Centre.
source_filename: Publication of Notification of the Code on Social Security 2020 regarding Career Centre.pdf

title: Notification under Section 83(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 (Setting up of the Worker Re-skilling Fund)
summary: This notification (S.O. 2316(E), 8 May 2026) establishes the Worker Re-skilling Fund for the purposes of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, which finances retraining of retrenched workers.
source_filename: Setting up of Reskilling Fund under section 83 (1) of IR Code 2020.pdf

title: Notification under Section 89(1) of the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 (Officers Empowered to Compound Offences)
summary: This notification (S.O. 2317(E), 8 May 2026) designates the Chief Labour Commissioner (Central), Additional and Deputy Chief Labour Commissioners across specified regional jurisdictions as the gazetted officers empowered to compound offences under the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 for establishments where the Central Government is the appropriate government.
source_filename: Specifying Gazetted Officers for Compounding of Offences under section 89(1) of the Industrial Relations Code 2020.pdf

title: Withdrawal of Board of Mining Examinations Bye-laws on Gas Testing Certificates under the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 (16 March 2026)
summary: This DGMS notification (G.S.R. 187(E), 16 March 2026) rescinds, with immediate effect, the Board of Mining Examinations bye-laws (G.S.R. 491(E) dated 22 July 2025) governing the grant of Gas Testing Certificates of Competency under Regulations 12(1) and 12(4) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.

title: Additional FAQs on the Labour Codes (as on 16 March 2026)
summary: This Ministry of Labour and Employment FAQ clarifies interpretive questions on the four Labour Codes, including how overtime, gratuity, PF contributions and statutory bonus are treated in computing the 50 percent wage floor under the Code on Wages, 2019, and wage-protection provisions for white-collar employees.

title: Booklet: New Labour Code for New India
summary: This government booklet presents the four Labour Codes as the biggest labour reforms since independence, explaining how they universalise minimum wages, ensure timely payment of wages, prioritise occupational safety, and extend social security while reducing compliance burden.
source_filename: Booklet- New Labour Code for New India.pdf

title: Compliance Handbook for Employers under the Four Labour Codes (Central Government Sphere)
summary: This handbook guides employers through their obligations under the Code on Wages 2019, the Industrial Relations Code 2020, the OSH&WC Code 2020 and the Code on Social Security 2020, with chapter-wise action points, key definitions and relevant schedules.

title: DGMS 03/2017 — Alert on Occurrence of Accidents
summary: Advises owners, agents and managers of coal mines to regularly monitor the "Alert/Hazard" menu on the DGMS website, where accident and incident alerts are uploaded, so remedial measures can be taken to prevent recurrence of similar accidents.

title: DGMS General Circular 01/2023 — Jurisdiction of Zones and Regions of DGMS after Reorganisation
summary: Sets out the revised jurisdiction of the zones and regional offices of DGMS following reorganisation notified vide G.S.R. 300(E) dated 17 April 2023, detailing the constitution of the Central Zone and its Dhanbad and Koderma regions across Bihar and Jharkhand.

title: DGMS (Legis.)(Exam) Circular 01/2024 — Issue of Statutory Certificates in Online/Digital Mode
summary: Informs stakeholders that the Board of Mining Examinations is issuing statutory certificates under the Coal Mines Regulations 2017, Metalliferous Mines Regulations 1961 and Mines Act 1952 on both examination and exemption basis in online/digital mode, and provides links to verify such certificates online.

title: DGMS Approval 01/2017 — Dust Suppression Device Approval for Metalliferous Mines
summary: Brings the approval of dust suppression/prevention devices fitted in drilling and boring equipment used in mines under a general approval scheme with finalized standards for their manufacture, testing and use, replacing case-by-case special orders.

title: DGMS Compiled Circulars 2015 (Combined)
summary: A compilation of DGMS circulars issued during 2015, opening with the Technical (MAMID) circular on accidents and incidents due to exposure to high atmospheric temperature and heat-related disorders (heat stroke, exhaustion, cramps and syncope) among mine workers.

title: DGMS Compiled Circulars 2016 (Combined)
summary: A compilation of DGMS circulars issued during 2016, opening with the general order permitting rescue stations and rescue rooms to maintain self-contained breathing apparatus of different types and makes under Rule 37 of the Mines Rescue Rules, 1985 subject to specified conditions.

title: DGMS notice — Online payment of examination fees under CMR 2017 and MMR 1961 (2018)
summary: Administrative notice on the online payment of competency-examination fees under the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 and Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961.

title: DGMS notice — Online payment of examination fees under CMR 2017 and MMR 1961 (2019)
summary: Administrative notice on the online payment of competency-examination fees under the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 and Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961.

title: DGMS General Circular 01/2020 — Uninterrupted Mineral Production and Transportation During COVID-19 Lockdown
summary: Conveys that coal and mineral production, transportation, supply of explosives and incidental mining operations are exempted from the COVID-19 lockdown shutdown per Ministry of Home Affairs guidelines, so mining operations may continue to sustain essential production during the pandemic.

title: DGMS General Circular 2025 — Young Safety Innovator Recognition Programme
summary: Invites nominations from students (under 30) and startups whose own innovations contributed to miners' safety, to be felicitated as Young Safety Innovators of new technologies at the 125th Foundation Day of DGMS on 7 January 2026 at Dhanbad.

title: DGMS Legislative Circular 02/2017 — Standards of Illumination in Opencast Coal Mines
summary: Reproduces, for strict compliance, the standards of illumination (in lux) to be provided at different work places in opencast coal mines under Regulation 154(2) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 1957, as specified vide notification G.S.R. 617(E) dated 28.04.2017.

title: DGMS Legislative Circular 03/2017 — Standards of Illumination in Opencast Metalliferous Mines
summary: Reproduces, for strict compliance, the standards of illumination (in lux) to be provided at different work places in opencast metalliferous mines under Regulation 148(2) of the Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961, as specified vide notification G.S.R. 618(E) dated 28.04.2017.

title: DGMS Legislative Circular 1/2018 — Standard for Emergency Escape Device Used in Oil Mines
summary: Prescribes the standard for the emergency escape device, escape line and slide required on every monkey board under Regulation 38(1) of the Oil Mines Regulations, 2017, covering manufacture, materials and construction so that persons can descend safely in an emergency.

title: DGMS Legislative (Tech.) Circular 2/2018 — Standard for Design, Construction, Installation and Testing of Lifting Appliances in Oil Mines
summary: Prescribes the standard for the design, construction, installation and testing of lifting appliances, gear and ropes used in oil mines under Regulation 105(2)(a) of the Oil Mines Regulations, 2017, referencing BIS, ISO, OISD and API specifications for equipment and wire ropes.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2019 — Safe conduct of operations in workover oil/gas mines
summary: Following a fatal fire at an onshore workover oil mine caused by a mobile-phone ignition of a hydrocarbon-gas cloud, this circular directs owners of oil/gas mines to provide separator vessels and flare stacks and to keep workover operations under statutory supervision, per Regulations 62(2)(c) and 129(7) of the Oil Mines Regulations, 2017.

title: DGMS (Tech)(SOMA) Circular 01 of 2026 — Preventive measures against rising mine accidents
summary: Analysing the 2022–2025 rise in fatal accidents (142/170 in coal, 144/184 in non-coal mines), this circular directs all mines to strengthen their Safety Management System against the leading causes — trackless machinery, HEMM, ground movement, explosives and falls from height.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2019 — Safety provisions for Diamond Wire Saw machines
summary: Prompted by a fatal accident in a marble mine from flying diamond beads off a snapped wire, this circular prescribes design, guarding and operating safety requirements for Diamond Wire Saw machines and ropes under Regulation 174(2) of the Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2019 — Safety provisions for Diamond Wire Saw machines (Metalliferous mines & OEMs)
summary: The formally-addressed version (dated 22 November 2019) of DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2019 to metalliferous-mine owners and diamond-wire-saw OEMs, setting out mandatory safety provisions for diamond wire saw machines after a fatal bead-projection accident.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2020 — Guidelines for systematic monitoring of opencast slopes
summary: Issued under Regulation 106(2) of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017, this circular standardises protocols for systematic slope-stability monitoring in opencast coal and metalliferous mines to enable timely withdrawal of men and machinery ahead of an impending slope failure.

title: DGMS (Tech)(SOMA) Circular 02 of 2026 — Heat wave and high-temperature exposure precautions
summary: Consolidating earlier heat-wave advisories, this circular directs all mines to protect workers from heat stress during peak summer, prescribing recognition of heat-illness symptoms and preventive measures against high-temperature accidents.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 03 of 2019 — Guidelines for implementation of Safety Management Plans
summary: This circular provides guidelines for implementing (not just formulating) statutory Safety Management Plans now mandated under CMR 2017 and OMR 2017, requiring auditable documentation of controls for each identified principal hazard.

title: DGMS (Tech)(OH) Circular 03 of 2025 — Heat wave and high-temperature exposure precautions
summary: Dated 28 April 2025, this circular reiterates precautions against heat-stress accidents in summer, describing the physiological effects of high atmospheric temperature (heat stroke, rash, sunburn, fatigue) and the preventive measures mines must strictly follow.

title: DGMS (Tech)(DMRS) Circular 04 of 2025 — Emergency response and evacuation plan for mine disasters
summary: Supplementing Regulation 252 of CMR 2017, Regulation 190A of MMR 1961 and Regulation 102 of OMR 2017, this circular requires constitution of Rapid Response Teams and manned rescue stations to handle fire and disaster emergencies in coal, metalliferous and oil mines.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 05 of 2020 — Safe conduct of drilling and production in oil and gas mines
summary: Responding to a spate of fatal hydrocarbon-gas ignition accidents at oil/gas installations, this circular frames guidelines for safe drilling and production operations, including weekly managerial supervision, defined HSE policy and controls against surcharged gas clouds.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 06 of 2020 — Minimum design requirements for safety features on HEMM and vehicles
summary: This circular prescribes minimum design requirements for safety features on Heavy Earth Moving Machinery and heavy/light vehicles in opencast mines, to address the rise in transport-machinery accidents under CMR 2017 and MMR 1961.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 07 of 2020 — Safe conduct of operations at mine railway sidings
summary: After accidents from inadvertent charging of 25kV traction lines, this circular mandates written permit-to-work and lock-out arrangements with railway authorities for loading, sampling and other work near overhead lines at mine railway sidings.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 07 of 2025 — Safety features in HEMM and auxiliary opencast equipment
summary: Citing 2025 statistics of dumper/tipper-related fatal accidents, this circular directs opencast coal and metalliferous mines and OEMs to ensure prescribed safety features on wheeled trackless transportation machinery and auxiliary equipment.

title: DGMS (Tech)(OH) Circular 01 of 2022 — Accidents due to exposure to high atmospheric temperature
summary: This circular alerts all mines to heat-related morbidity and mortality during the 2022 heat waves and re-circulates the 2015 MAMID guidance on precautionary measures against high-atmospheric-temperature accidents.

title: DGMS (Tech)(OH) Circular 01 of 2023 — Accidents due to exposure to high temperature
summary: Dated 24 April 2023, this circular requires mine managements to educate officers, supervisors and workmen on heat-related illnesses and first aid, describing the physiological effects of working in high atmospheric temperature.

title: DGMS (Tech)(OH) Circular 01 of 2024 — Heat wave precautions against high-temperature accidents
summary: Dated 19 April 2024, this circular sets out heat-stress symptom recognition, do's and don'ts for treating affected miners, and administrative controls (hydration, task scheduling, protective clothing) for the summer season.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 11 of 2020 — Online submission of annual returns
summary: Administrative circular notifying mines that annual returns can now be filed online through the DGMS website software module, with instructions for retrospective filing from 2015 onwards.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 12 (Revised) of 2020 — Online submission of accident notices
summary: This revised circular enables and mandates online real-time reporting of accident intimations and notices of accidents and dangerous occurrences (Forms IVA/IVB/IVC) through the DGMS website software module from 1 August 2020.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2022 — Recommendations of the Rajmahal Court of Inquiry
summary: This circular conveys the recommendations of the Court of Inquiry into the 2016 Rajmahal opencast dump-failure that buried 23 workers, covering project-report approval, land acquisition, and segregation of planning and execution authorities.

title: FAQ on Myths and Realities of the Industrial Relations (IR) Code, 2020 (23 December 2025)
summary: This FAQ rebuts common misconceptions about the IR Code, 2020, clarifying that trade-union registration and the right to strike (with 14 days' notice) are retained, that retrenchment protections and compensation continue, and that prior government permission for retrenchment applies to establishments with 300 or more workers.
source_filename: FAQ on Myths and Realities of Industrial Relation (IR) Code 2020 dated 23-12-2025.pdf

title: FAQ on the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (OSH&WC) Code, 2020 (as on 13 March 2026)
summary: This FAQ clarifies the scope of the OSH&WC Code, 2020, covering applicability of central rules to dock workers at non-major ports, free pre-employment and periodic health examinations, and the division of rule-making authority between the Central and State Governments.

title: FAQs on the Code on Wages, 2019 (24 December 2025)
summary: This FAQ explains key features of the Code on Wages, 2019, including its universal applicability across all employees, bonus eligibility for daily-wage workers, the floor-wage baseline, the revised wage definition, and the cap on total deductions at 50 percent of wages.

title: FAQs on the Labour Codes (30 December 2025)
summary: This FAQ explains transitional application of old rules, the definition of "wages" and its components, the 50 percent allowance rule, the treatment of leave encashment, and provides worked illustrations of how allowances are added back to wages under the Labour Codes.

title: FAQs on the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (OSH&WC) Code, 2020 (19 December 2025)
summary: This FAQ addresses concerns about the OSH&WC Code, 2020, clarifying that raising the factory and contract-labour thresholds does not remove worker protections, that 12-hour daily flexibility is subject to overtime at twice the wage, that night work for women requires consent and safeguards, and that inspector-cum-facilitators retain enforcement accountability.
source_filename: FAQs on Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (OSH&WC) Code, 2020 dated 19-12-2025.pdf

title: FAQs on the Code on Social Security, 2020 (9 January 2026)
summary: This FAQ clarifies the Social Security Rules, covering optional digital versus physical submission of claims, registration as an entry point to schemes, amendment of rules by notification, mandatory separate maintenance and CAG audit of Social Security Funds, and protection of maternity-benefit claims against procedural rejection.
source_filename: FAQs on Social Security Code dated 09-01-2026.pdf

title: Jharkhand Platform Based Gig Workers (Registration and Welfare) Bill, 2024
summary: This Jharkhand Gazette notification (1 July 2024) publishes the draft Platform Based Gig Workers (Registration and Welfare) Bill, 2024, which proposes registration of gig workers and platforms, a Welfare Board and welfare fund, and obligations on platforms for social security, occupational safety, algorithmic transparency and dispute resolution.

title: Key Provisions under the Code on Social Security, 2020 (MoLE Circular to States/UTs, 4 January 2026)
summary: This Ministry of Labour and Employment communication to State and UT Labour Departments highlights the enforcement of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (from 21 November 2025) and identifies the sections under which the Central Government, State Governments and Appropriate Governments are to frame rules and schemes.
source_filename: Key provisions under the Code on Social Security, 2020.pdf

title: DGMS Safety Alert 09/2025 — Fatal fall from height at ONGC WD-51 Workover Oil Well Mine, Gujarat
summary: Reports a fatal 20 m fall of a contractual topman climbing a workover-rig ladder at ONGC's WD-51 mine and recommends full-body harnesses, secured ladders, and guarded monkey boards to prevent recurrence.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 10/2025 — Fatal blasting-projectile accident at Gare Palma IV/2&3 Opencast Coal Mine, Chhattisgarh

title: DGMS Safety Alert 11/2025 — Fatal accident from fall of person handling water hose of twin bolter machine, Churcha Mine (SECL)
summary: Reports a fatal accident at Churcha Mine (SECL), Korea district, Chhattisgarh, where a contractor worker handling the water hose of a stuck twin bolter machine during recovery was struck by its rear side, and recommends a detailed SOP for recovery of stuck machinery, demarcation of danger zones and use of hydraulic equipment retrievers.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 12/2025 — Fatal accident from overturning of employee bus at Dulanga Coal Mine (NTPC)
summary: Reports a fatal accident at Dulanga Coal Mining Project (NTPC), Sundargarh, Odisha, where a bus carrying mine employees overturned killing a person standing at an open door, and recommends automatic door-locking, driver rest periods, road maintenance and pre-trip fitness checks for LMV transport.

summary: Reports a driller killed when about 104 cubic metres of loose overhang boulders fell 24 metres onto a drilling crew and recommends geotechnical inspections, removal of overhangs, proper bench angles and pre-shift highwall checks.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 18/2025 - Fatal side-fall of two outsiders at Gevra Opencast Mine (SECL)
summary: Reports two unauthorised outsiders killed by a side fall while picking coal from an abandoned seam at Gevra OC Mine and recommends covering exposed abandoned seams, effective peripheral fencing/trenching and regular inspection to prevent unauthorised entry.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 19/2025 - Fatal electrocution at Gevra Opencast Mine (SECL)
summary: Reports an electrical helper electrocuted during cable-joint checking in the coal handling plant's PQ circuit and recommends avoiding temporary cable joints, lock-out/tag-out shutdowns, use of non-contact line detectors and a trained first-aider on site.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 20/2025 - Fatal fall of dumper at Sri Surya Granite Mine, Andhra Pradesh
summary: Reports two workmen killed and two seriously injured when a dumper carrying riders lost control on a steep haul road and fell between benches, and recommends limiting haul-road gradients to 1 in 16, banning riding on dumpers and providing parapet walls/berms.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 21/2025 - Fatal haul-road accident (hit by empty truck) at Gevra Opencast Mine (SECL)
summary: Reports a driller's helper fatally struck by an empty truck while crossing a haul road at Gevra OC Mine and recommends barring pedestrians from haul roads and providing designated crossing points.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 10/2024 - Fatal high-bench collapse claiming four lives at Pahara Stone Mine, Uttar Pradesh
summary: Reports four drillers killed when a 33m x 53m section separated from a 73-metre high bench at an opencast stone mine and recommends geotechnical controls, safe bench design, SOP compliance and competent supervision.

title: DGMS Safety Alert (23.06.2021) - Fatal tyre-rim accident at Sonepur Bazari Opencast Project (ECL)
summary: Reports two workers killed at Sonepur Bazari OC mine when a wheel rim flew off a tyre gripped by a tyre handler while they were removing wheel nuts.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 8/2025 - Fatal roof fall at Chhatarpur Mine No.1 (Western Coalfields), Madhya Pradesh
summary: Reports an Assistant Manager, Overman and Mining Sirdar killed when a sandstone roof layer fell over them at an unsupported development face and recommends visual/sounding roof inspection, banning work under unsupported roof and strict support-density compliance.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 26/2025 - Fatal run-over by loading machine at Kamptee Deep OC Mine (Western Coalfields)
summary: Reports a loader operator run over by his own excavator at a sand bench and recommends safe parking/mounting practices, exclusion zones around the machine and full 360-degree operator visibility.

title: DGMS Safety Alert 27/2025 - Fatal fall-of-object at Daroli Limestone Mine (Udaipur Cement Works)
summary: Reports a fitter buried and killed when a tipper unloaded limestone into a crusher hopper during grizzly maintenance work below it and recommends effective barriers to prevent unloading during maintenance and thorough supervision.
